  "description": "Run ARC replacer tests"
}
Output
"Error: Exit code 1\nRunning main() from gmock_main.cc\n[==========] Running 2 tests from 1 test suite.\n[----------] Global test environment set-up.\n[----------] 2 tests from ArcReplacerTest\n[ RUN      ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest\n/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp:56: Failure\nExpected equality of these values:\n  2\n  arc_replacer.Evict()\n    Which is: (5)\n\n[  FAILED  ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest (0 ms)\n[ RUN      ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest2\n/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp:118: Failure\nExpected equality of these values:\n  1\n  arc_replacer.Evict()\n    Which is: (3)\n\n[  FAILED  ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest2 (0 ms)\n[----------] 2 tests from ArcReplacerTest (0 ms total)\n\n[----------] Global test environment tear-down\n[==========] 2 tests from 1 test suite ran. (0 ms total)\n[  PASSED  ] 0 tests.\n[  FAILED  ] 2 tests, listed below:\n[  FAILED  ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest\n[  FAILED  ] ArcReplacerTest.DISABLED_SampleTest2\n\n 2 FAILED TESTS"
128. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer_test.cpp\n//\n// Identification: test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n/**\n * arc_replacer_test.cpp\n */\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n\n#include \"gtest/gtest.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\nTEST(ArcReplacerTest, DISABLED_SampleTest) {\n  // for the sake of simplicity\n  // we use (a, fb) to notate page a on frame b,\n  // (a, _) to mark ghost page with page id a\n  // p(a, fb) to mark pinned page a on frame b\n  // we use [<-mru_ghost-][<-mru-]![-mfu->][->mfu_ghost->] p=x\n  // to denote the 4 lists where pages closer to ! is are more fresh\n  // and record the mru target size as x\n  ArcReplacer arc_replacer(7);\n  // Add six frames to the replacer.\n  // We set frame 6 as non-evictable. These pages all go to mru list\n  // We now have frames [][(1,f1), (2,f2), (3,f3), (4,f4), (5,f5), p(6,f6)]![][]\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 2);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 3);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(4, 4);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(5, 5);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(6, 6);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(4, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(5, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(6, false);\n\n  // The size of the replacer is the number of frames that can be evicted, _not_ the total number of frames entered.\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Record an access for frame 1. Now frame 1 goes into mfu list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  // Now [][(2,f2), (3,f3), (4,f4), (5,f5), p(6,f6)]![(1,f1)][] p=0\n  //\n  // Now Evict three pages from the replacer.\n  // Since target size is still 0, mru side should be evicted\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(4, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(2,_), (3,_), (4,_)][(5,f5), p(6,f6)]![(1,f1)][] p=0\n\n  // Insert new page 7 on frame 2, this should NOT be a hit on ghost\n  // list since we've never seen page 7, this goes into mru list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 7);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  // Insert page 2 on frame 3, this should be a hit on mru ghost list\n  // since we've just evicted page 2, this goes into mfu list\n  // also target size should be bumped up by 1, since mru ghost has\n  // size 3 and mfu ghost has size 0\n  // starting with x is pageid on ghost list, starting with _ is pinned\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [(3,_), (4,_)][(5,f5), p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=1\n  ASSERT_EQ(4, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // Continue to insert page 3 on frame 4 and page 4 on frame 7\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(4, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(4, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(7, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(7, true);\n  // Now [][(5,f5), p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=3\n  ASSERT_EQ(6, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // Evict an entry, now target size is 3, we still evict from mru\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=3\n  // Evict another entry, this time mru is smaller than target,\n  // mfu is victimized\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Make another access to page 1 on frame 5, now page 1 is back to mfu\n  // with a different frame, also p is adjusted down by 1/1=1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(5, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(5, true);\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(1,f5), (4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][] p=2\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // We evict again, this time target size is 2, we evict from mru,\n  // note that page 6 is pinned. Victim is page 7\n  // Now [(5,_), (7,_)][p(6,f6)]![(1,f5), (4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][] p=2\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n}\n\nTEST(ArcReplacerTest, DISABLED_SampleTest2) {\n  // Test a smaller capacity\n  ArcReplacer arc_replacer(3);\n  // Fill up the replacer\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [][(1,f1), (2,f2), (3,f3)]![][] p=0\n  // Evict all pages\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(1,_), (2,_), (3,_)][]![][] p=0\n\n  // Insert a new page 4 with frame 3. This is case 4A\n  // and ghost pages 1 should be driven out\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [(2,_), (3,_)][(4,f3)]![][] p=0\n\n  // Access page 1 on frame 2, it should NOT be a hit on\n  // the ghost list. Ghost page 2 should be driven out\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(3,_)][(4,f3), (1,f2)]![][] p=0\n\n  // Access page 3 with frame 1, this should be a ghost hit,\n  // page 3 is placed on mfu and target size is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [][(4,f3), (1,f2)]![(3,f1)][] p=1\n\n  // Make some more ghosts by evicting all pages again\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(4,_), (1,_)][]![][(3,_)] p=1\n\n  // Let's make even more ghost to fill the list to \"full\"\n  // Insert page 1 again so it goes to mfu side,\n  // target is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [(4,_)][]![(1,f1)][(3,_)] p=2\n\n  // Insert page 4 again so it goes to mfu side,\n  // target is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  // Now [][]![(4,f2),(1,f1)][(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now insert and evict one new page at a time\n  // Insert page 5 and evict, since target size is 3,\n  // should victimize page 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 5);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(5,f3)]![(4,f2)][(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n  // Insert page 6 and evict, notice target size is 3,\n  // so page 4 gets evicted\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 6);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(5,f3),(6,f1)]![(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n  // Insert page 7 and evict, notice target size is 3,\n  // so page 5 gets evicted\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 7);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![][(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now the list is full! reaching 2*capacity\n  // adjust page 5 to mfu list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 5);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![(5,f3)][(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now evict, target should be mfu\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now mru and mru_ghost together has\n  // less than 3 records. When inserting a new page 2\n  // this should be case 4B and\n  // four lists total size equals 2 * capacity case,\n  // So mfu ghost will be shrinked\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2),(2,f3)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Evict a page 6\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(6,_)][(7,f2),(2,f3)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n  // And access page 3 who was removed\n  // then this is case 4A, ghost page 6 will be removed\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [][(7,f2),(2,f3),(3,f1)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Finally we evict all pages and see if the order is right,\n  // note that target size is 3\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n}\n\n// Feel free to write more tests!\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp",
    "numLines": 220,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 220
  },
  "type": "text"
}
129. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // No evictable entries\n  if (curr_size_ == 0) {\n    return std::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Helper to evict from a list and move to ghost\n  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &src_list, std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,\n                              ArcStatus ghost_status, frame_id_t frame_id) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    page_id_t page_id = status->page_id_;\n\n    // Remove from source list\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      src_list.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n    } else {\n      src_list.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n    }\n\n    // Add to ghost list (front)\n    ghost_list.push_front(page_id);\n\n    // Update ghost map\n    auto ghost_status_ptr = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false, ghost_status);\n    ghost_status_ptr->ghost_iter_ = ghost_list.begin();\n    ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status_ptr;\n\n    // Remove from alive map\n    alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n    // Trim ghost list if needed (should not exceed c)\n    while (ghost_list.size() > replacer_size_) {\n      auto old_page = ghost_list.back();\n      ghost_list.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n\n    curr_size_--;\n  };\n\n  // Decide which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_\n  bool evict_from_mru = (mru_.size() > mru_target_size_) || (mru_.size() == mru_target_size_ && !mru_.empty());\n\n  // If mru is empty, we must evict from mfu\n  if (mru_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = false;\n  }\n  // If mfu is empty, we must evict from mru\n  if (mfu_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = true;\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from the preferred list first\n  if (evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from mfu\n  if (!mfu_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mfu_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_, ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If we couldn't evict from preferred list, try the other list\n  if (!evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return std::nullopt;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (alive map)\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) > 0) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    // Move to front of mfu_ (promote to frequently used)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru_\n      mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;\n    } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n      // Already in mfu_, move to front\n      mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n    }\n    // Update page_id if it changed\n    status->page_id_ = page_id;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_\n  if (ghost_map_.count(page_id) > 0) {\n    auto ghost_status = ghost_map_[page_id];\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {\n      // Increase mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);\n\n      // Remove from mru_ghost_\n      mru_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {\n      // Decrease mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      if (mru_target_size_ >= delta) {\n        mru_target_size_ -= delta;\n      } else {\n        mru_target_size_ = 0;\n      }\n\n      // Remove from mfu_ghost_\n      mfu_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n  bool was_evictable = status->evictable_;\n\n  if (was_evictable && !set_evictable) {\n    // Was evictable, now not evictable\n    curr_size_--;\n  } else if (!was_evictable && set_evictable) {\n    // Was not evictable, now evictable\n    curr_size_++;\n  }\n\n  status->evictable_ = set_evictable;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n\n  // If not evictable, throw an exception\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(status->evictable_, \"Remove called on non-evictable frame\");\n\n  // Remove from the appropriate list\n  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n    mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n    mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n  }\n\n  // Remove from alive map\n  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n  // Decrement size\n  curr_size_--;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n  return curr_size_;\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
    "numLines": 344,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 344

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "new_string": "  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list (from back - LRU order)\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    // Iterate from back to front (oldest to newest)\n    for (auto it = list.rbegin(); it != list.rend(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "newString": "  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list (from back - LRU order)\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    // Iterate from back to front (oldest to newest)\n    for (auto it = list.rbegin(); it != list.rend(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };",
  "oldString": "  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };",
  "originalFile": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // No evictable entries\n  if (curr_size_ == 0) {\n    return std::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Helper to evict from a list and move to ghost\n  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &src_list, std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,\n                              ArcStatus ghost_status, frame_id_t frame_id) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    page_id_t page_id = status->page_id_;\n\n    // Remove from source list\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      src_list.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n    } else {\n      src_list.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n    }\n\n    // Add to ghost list (front)\n    ghost_list.push_front(page_id);\n\n    // Update ghost map\n    auto ghost_status_ptr = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false, ghost_status);\n    ghost_status_ptr->ghost_iter_ = ghost_list.begin();\n    ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status_ptr;\n\n    // Remove from alive map\n    alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n    // Trim ghost list if needed (should not exceed c)\n    while (ghost_list.size() > replacer_size_) {\n      auto old_page = ghost_list.back();\n      ghost_list.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n\n    curr_size_--;\n  };\n\n  // Decide which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_\n  bool evict_from_mru = (mru_.size() > mru_target_size_) || (mru_.size() == mru_target_size_ && !mru_.empty());\n\n  // If mru is empty, we must evict from mfu\n  if (mru_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = false;\n  }\n  // If mfu is empty, we must evict from mru\n  if (mfu_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = true;\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from the preferred list first\n  if (evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from mfu\n  if (!mfu_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mfu_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_, ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If we couldn't evict from preferred list, try the other list\n  if (!evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return std::nullopt;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (alive map)\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) > 0) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    // Move to front of mfu_ (promote to frequently used)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru_\n      mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;\n    } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n      // Already in mfu_, move to front\n      mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n    }\n    // Update page_id if it changed\n    status->page_id_ = page_id;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_\n  if (ghost_map_.count(page_id) > 0) {\n    auto ghost_status = ghost_map_[page_id];\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {\n      // Increase mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);\n\n      // Remove from mru_ghost_\n      mru_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {\n      // Decrease mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      if (mru_target_size_ >= delta) {\n        mru_target_size_ -= delta;\n      } else {\n        mru_target_size_ = 0;\n      }\n\n      // Remove from mfu_ghost_\n      mfu_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n  bool was_evictable = status->evictable_;\n\n  if (was_evictable && !set_evictable) {\n    // Was evictable, now not evictable\n    curr_size_--;\n  } else if (!was_evictable && set_evictable) {\n    // Was not evictable, now evictable\n    curr_size_++;\n  }\n\n  status->evictable_ = set_evictable;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n\n  // If not evictable, throw an exception\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(status->evictable_, \"Remove called on non-evictable frame\");\n\n  // Remove from the appropriate list\n  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n    mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n    mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n  }\n\n  // Remove from alive map\n  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n  // Decrement size\n  curr_size_--;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n  return curr_size_;\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

{

  "new_string": "  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Case 4A: If mru + mru_ghost >= c, remove oldest from mru_ghost\n  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {\n    if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n      auto old_page = mru_ghost_.back();\n      mru_ghost_.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n  } else if (mru_.size() + mfu_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() >= 2 * replacer_size_) {\n    // Case 4B: If total >= 2c, remove oldest from mfu_ghost\n    if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n      auto old_page = mfu_ghost_.back();\n      mfu_ghost_.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}",
  "old_string": "  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "newString": "  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Case 4A: If mru + mru_ghost >= c, remove oldest from mru_ghost\n  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {\n    if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n      auto old_page = mru_ghost_.back();\n      mru_ghost_.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n  } else if (mru_.size() + mfu_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() >= 2 * replacer_size_) {\n    // Case 4B: If total >= 2c, remove oldest from mfu_ghost\n    if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n      auto old_page = mfu_ghost_.back();\n      mfu_ghost_.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}",
  "oldString": "  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}",
  "originalFile": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // No evictable entries\n  if (curr_size_ == 0) {\n    return std::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list (from back - LRU order)\n  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    // Iterate from back to front (oldest to newest)\n    for (auto it = list.rbegin(); it != list.rend(); ++it) {\n      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];\n      if (status->evictable_) {\n        return *it;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Helper to evict from a list and move to ghost\n  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &src_list, std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,\n                              ArcStatus ghost_status, frame_id_t frame_id) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    page_id_t page_id = status->page_id_;\n\n    // Remove from source list\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      src_list.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n    } else {\n      src_list.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n    }\n\n    // Add to ghost list (front)\n    ghost_list.push_front(page_id);\n\n    // Update ghost map\n    auto ghost_status_ptr = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false, ghost_status);\n    ghost_status_ptr->ghost_iter_ = ghost_list.begin();\n    ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status_ptr;\n\n    // Remove from alive map\n    alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n    // Trim ghost list if needed (should not exceed c)\n    while (ghost_list.size() > replacer_size_) {\n      auto old_page = ghost_list.back();\n      ghost_list.pop_back();\n      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n    }\n\n    curr_size_--;\n  };\n\n  // Decide which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_\n  bool evict_from_mru = (mru_.size() > mru_target_size_) || (mru_.size() == mru_target_size_ && !mru_.empty());\n\n  // If mru is empty, we must evict from mfu\n  if (mru_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = false;\n  }\n  // If mfu is empty, we must evict from mru\n  if (mfu_.empty()) {\n    evict_from_mru = true;\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from the preferred list first\n  if (evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Try to evict from mfu\n  if (!mfu_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mfu_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_, ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // If we couldn't evict from preferred list, try the other list\n  if (!evict_from_mru && !mru_.empty()) {\n    auto victim = find_evictable_in_list(mru_);\n    if (victim.has_value()) {\n      evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST, victim.value());\n      return victim;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return std::nullopt;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (alive map)\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) > 0) {\n    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n    // Move to front of mfu_ (promote to frequently used)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru_\n      mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;\n    } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n      // Already in mfu_, move to front\n      mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n    }\n    // Update page_id if it changed\n    status->page_id_ = page_id;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_\n  if (ghost_map_.count(page_id) > 0) {\n    auto ghost_status = ghost_map_[page_id];\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {\n      // Increase mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);\n\n      // Remove from mru_ghost_\n      mru_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {\n      // Decrease mru_target_size_\n      size_t delta = std::max(mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),\n                              static_cast<size_t>(1));\n      if (mru_target_size_ >= delta) {\n        mru_target_size_ -= delta;\n      } else {\n        mru_target_size_ = 0;\n      }\n\n      // Remove from mfu_ghost_\n      mfu_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to front of mfu_\n      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)\n  // Add to front of mru_\n  mru_.push_front(frame_id);\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n  bool was_evictable = status->evictable_;\n\n  if (was_evictable && !set_evictable) {\n    // Was evictable, now not evictable\n    curr_size_--;\n  } else if (!was_evictable && set_evictable) {\n    // Was not evictable, now evictable\n    curr_size_++;\n  }\n\n  status->evictable_ = set_evictable;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Frame not found - return silently\n  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];\n\n  // If not evictable, throw an exception\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(status->evictable_, \"Remove called on non-evictable frame\");\n\n  // Remove from the appropriate list\n  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n    mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);\n  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n    mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);\n  }\n\n  // Remove from alive map\n  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n\n  // Decrement size\n  curr_size_--;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n  return curr_size_;\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   }",
        " ",
        "   // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)",
        "+  // Case 4A: If mru + mru_ghost >= c, remove oldest from mru_ghost",
        "+  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {",
        "+    if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {",
        "+      auto old_page = mru_ghost_.back();",
        "+      mru_ghost_.pop_back();",
        "+      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);",
        "+    }",
        "+  } else if (mru_.size() + mfu_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() >= 2 * replacer_size_) {",
        "+    // Case 4B: If total >= 2c, remove oldest from mfu_ghost",
        "+    if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {",
        "+      auto old_page = mfu_ghost_.back();",
        "+      mfu_ghost_.pop_back();",
        "+      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);",
        "+    }",
        "+  }",
        "+",
        "   // Add to front of mru_",
        "   mru_.push_front(frame_id);",

DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");

  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });

After
DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });
}
2. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });
}
After
DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });
}
3. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {}
After
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {
  for (auto &request : requests) {
    request_queue_.Put(std::move(request));
  }
}
4. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {}
After
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {
  while (true) {
    auto request_opt = request_queue_.Get();
    // If we receive std::nullopt, it means we should exit
    if (!request_opt.has_value()) {
      break;
    }
    auto &request = request_opt.value();
    if (request.is_write_) {
      disk_manager_->WritePage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    } else {
      disk_manager_->ReadPage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    }
    // Signal completion
    request.callback_.set_value(true);
  }
}

  frame_id_t frame_id_;

      : page_id_(pid), frame_id_(fid), evictable_(ev), arc_status_(st) {}
};
After
// TODO(student): You can modify or remove this struct as you like.
struct FrameStatus {
  page_id_t page_id_;
  frame_id_t frame_id_;
  bool evictable_;
  ArcStatus arc_status_;
  std::list<frame_id_t>::iterator mru_iter_;
  std::list<frame_id_t>::iterator mfu_iter_;
  std::list<page_id_t>::iterator ghost_iter_;
  FrameStatus(page_id_t pid, frame_id_t fid, bool ev, ArcStatus st)
      : page_id_(pid), frame_id_(fid), evictable_(ev), arc_status_(st) {}
};
6. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // No evictable entries
  if (curr_size_ == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list
  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];
      if (status->evictable_) {
        return *it;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };

  // Helper to evict from a list and move to ghost
  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &src_list, std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,
                              ArcStatus ghost_status, frame_id_t frame_id) {
    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];
    page_id_t page_id = status->page_id_;

    // Remove from source list
    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
      src_list.erase(status->mru_iter_);
    } else {
      src_list.erase(status->mfu_iter_);
    }

    // Add to ghost list (front)
    ghost_list.push_front(page_id);

    // Update ghost map
    auto ghost_status_ptr = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false, ghost_status);
    ghost_status_ptr->ghost_iter_ = ghost_list.begin();
    ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status_ptr;

    // Remove from alive map
    alive_map_.erase(frame_id);

    // Trim ghost list if needed (should not exceed c)
    while (ghost_list.size() > replacer_size_) {
      auto old_page = ghost_list.back();
      ghost_list.pop_back();
      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);
    }

    curr_size_--;
  };

  // Decide which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_
  bool evict_from_mru = (mru_.size() > mru_target_size_) || (mru_.size() == mru_target_size_ && !mru_.empty());

  // If mru is
... (truncated)

auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // No evictable entries
  if (curr_size_ == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list
  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];
      if (status->evictable_) {
        return *it;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };

  // Helper to evict from a list and move to ghost
  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &src_list, std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,
                              ArcStatus ghost_status, frame_id_t frame_id) {
    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];
    page_id_t page_id = status->page_id_;

    // Remove from alive map

8. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (alive map)
  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) > 0) {
    auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];
    // Move to front of mfu_ (promote to frequently used)
    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
      // Remove from mru_
      mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);
      // Add to front of mfu_
      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);
      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();
      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;
    } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {
      // Already in mfu_, move to front
      mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);
      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);
      status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();
    }
    // Update page_id if it changed
    status->page_id_ = page_id;
    return;
  }

  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_
  if (ghost_map_.count(page_id) > 0) {
    auto ghost_status = ghost_map_[page_id];
    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {
      // Increase mru_target_size_
      size_t delta = std::max(mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)),
                              static_cast<size_t>(1));
      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);

      // Remove from mru_ghost_
      mru_ghost_.erase(ghost_status->ghost_iter_);
      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);

      // Add to front of mfu_
      mfu_.push_front(frame_id);
      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);
      new_status->mfu_iter_ = mfu_.begin();
      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;
      return;
    }

    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_
    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ =
... (truncated)
9. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Frame not found - return silently
  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {
    return;
  }

  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];
  bool was_evictable = status->evictable_;

  if (was_evictable && !set_evictable) {
    // Was evictable, now not evictable
    curr_size_--;
  } else if (!was_evictable && set_evictable) {
    // Was not evictable, now evictable
    curr_size_++;
  }

  status->evictable_ = set_evictable;
}
10. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Frame not found - return silently
  if (alive_map_.count(frame_id) == 0) {
    return;
  }

  auto status = alive_map_[frame_id];

  // If not evictable, throw an exception
  BUSTUB_ENSURE(status->evictable_, "Remove called on non-evictable frame");

  // Remove from the appropriate list
  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
    mru_.erase(status->mru_iter_);
  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {
    mfu_.erase(status->mfu_iter_);
  }

  // Remove from alive map
  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);

  // Decrement size
  curr_size_--;
}
11. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);
  return curr_size_;
}

ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // Acquire shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.lock_shared();
}
13. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept {}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept
    : page_id_(that.page_id_),
      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),
      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),
      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {
  // Invalidate the source guard
  that.is_valid_ = false;
}
14. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }
After
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {
  if (this == &that) {
    return *this;
  }

  // Drop current resources if valid
  Drop();

  // Move from source
  page_id_ = that.page_id_;
  frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);
  replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);
  bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);
  disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);
  is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;

  // Invalidate source
  that.is_valid_ = false;

  return *this;
}
15. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() {
  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, "tried to flush an invalid read guard");

  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    requests.push_back({true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;
  }
}
16. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the shared lock
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    auto pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
    if (pin_count == 1) {
      // Pin count is now 0, set as evictable
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
    }
  }

  // Invalidate this guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
17. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // Acquire exclusive lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.lock();
  // Mark the page as dirty since we're writing to it
  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;
}

auto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }

23. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  // If the page is not in the buffer pool, just deallocate it on disk
  if (page_table_.count(page_id) == 0) {
    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);
    return true;
  }

  // Get the frame holding this page
  frame_id_t frame_id = page_table_[page_id];
  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];

  // If the page is pinned, we can't delete it
  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {
    return false;
  }

  // Remove from page table
  page_table_.erase(page_id);

  // Remove from replacer
  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);

  // Reset the frame and add back to free list
  frame->Reset();
  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);

  // Deallocate on disk
  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);

  return true;
}

Before
auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;
  frame_id_t frame_id;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool
    if (page_table_.count(page_id) > 0) {
      frame_id = page_table_[page_id];
      frame = frames_[frame_id];
      // Increment pin count and set not evictable
      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);
      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);
      return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);
    }

    // Case 2: Try to get a free frame
    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {
      frame_id = free_frames_.front();
      free_frames_.pop_front();
      frame = frames_[frame_id];
    } else {
      // Case 3: Need to evict a frame
      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();
      if (!evicted.has_value()) {
        // No frame can be evicted
        return std::nullopt;
      }
      frame_id = evicted.value();
      frame = frames_[frame_id];

      // Find the page that was in this frame and remove from page table
      for (auto it = page_table_.begin(); it != page_table_.end(); ++it) {
        if (it->second == frame_id) {
          // If dirty, flush to disk first
          if (frame->is_dirty_) {
            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
            auto future = promise.get_future();
            requests.push_back({true, frame->GetDataMut(), it->first, std::move(promise)});
            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
            future.get();
          }
          page_table_.erase(it);
          break;
        }
      }
    }

      if (!evicted.has_value()) {
        // No frame can be evicted
        return std::nullopt;

      frame_id = evicted.value();
      frame = frames_[frame_id];

      // Find the page that was in this frame and remove from page table

26. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  // Page not in buffer pool
  if (page_table_.count(page_id) == 0) {
    return false;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = page_table_[page_id];
  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];

  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    requests.push_back({true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
27. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

    // Page not in buffer pool
    if (page_table_.count(page_id) == 0) {
      return false;
    }

    frame_id_t frame_id = page_table_[page_id];
    frame = frames_[frame_id];
  }

  // Take shared lock on the frame before flushing
  std::shared_lock lock(frame->rwlatch_);

  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    requests.push_back({true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
28. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
    auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      requests.push_back({true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();
      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
29. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() {
  std::vector<std::pair<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader>>> pages_to_flush;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
      pages_to_flush.push_back({page_id, frames_[frame_id]});
    }
  }

  for (auto &[page_id, frame] : pages_to_flush) {
    std::shared_lock lock(frame->rwlatch_);
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      requests.push_back({true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();
      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
30. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  // Page not in buffer pool
  if (page_table_.count(page_id) == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = page_table_[page_id];
  return frames_[frame_id]->pin_count_.load();
}

auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;
  frame_id_t frame_id;
  bool page_already_in_buffer = false;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool

    } else {

        free_frames_.pop_front();

33. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
      // Reset frame and set up for new page
    frame->Reset();
    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;

    // Pin the frame and record access
    frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);
    replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);
  }

  // Read the page data from disk (outside the bpm lock)
  std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
  auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
  auto future = promise.get_future();
  requests.push_back({false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
  disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
  future.get();

  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);
}
After
      // Reset frame and set up for new page
      frame->Reset();
      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;

      // Pin the frame and record access
      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);
      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);
    }
  }  // Release bpm_latch here before acquiring frame latch

  // If page was not already in buffer, read from disk
  if (!page_already_in_buffer) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    requests.push_back({false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
  }

  // Now create the guard which will acquire the frame's shared latch
  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);
}
34. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list
  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];
      if (status->evictable_) {
        return *it;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };
After
  // Helper lambda to find an evictable frame in a list (from back - LRU order)
  auto find_evictable_in_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    // Iterate from back to front (oldest to newest)
    for (auto it = list.rbegin(); it != list.rend(); ++it) {
      auto &status = alive_map_[*it];
      if (status->evictable_) {
        return *it;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };
35. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)
  // Add to front of mru_
  mru_.push_front(frame_id);
  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);
  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();
  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;
}
After
  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists (new page)
  // Case 4A: If mru + mru_ghost >= c, remove oldest from mru_ghost
  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {
    if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {
      auto old_page = mru_ghost_.back();
      mru_ghost_.pop_back();
      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);
    }
  } else if (mru_.size() + mfu_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() >= 2 * replacer_size_) {
    // Case 4B: If total >= 2c, remove oldest from mfu_ghost
    if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {
      auto old_page = mfu_ghost_.back();
      mfu_ghost_.pop_back();
      ghost_map_.erase(old_page);
    }
  }

  // Add to front of mru_
  mru_.push_front(frame_id);
  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);
  new_status->mru_iter_ = mru_.begin();
  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;
}

      

      

        Bash Commands (65)
        
1. $ git clone http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/bustub.git 2>&1
2. $ curl -s --max-time 10 http://the-agent-company.com:8929/ 2>&1 || echo "Connection test failed"
3. $ ls -la /workspace/
4. $ cat /etc/hosts | grep -i agent || echo "No agent company entry"
5. $ ping -c 2 the-agent-company.com 2>&1 || echo "Ping failed"
6. $ timeout 30 git clone http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/bustub.git /workspace/bustub 2>&1
7. $ rm -rf /workspace/bustub 2>/dev/null; curl -s --max-time 15 http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/bustub 2>&1 || echo "Connection to GitLab failed"
8. $ nslookup the-agent-company.com 2>&1 | head -20
9. $ host the-agent-company.com 2>&1 || getent hosts the-agent-company.com
10. $ ip addr show 2>&1 | head -30
11. $ ifconfig 2>&1 | head -30 || cat /proc/net/route 2>&1 | head -20
12. $ cat /etc/resolv.conf
13. $ curl -v --connect-timeout 10 http://the-agent-company.com:8929/ 2>&1 | head -30
14. $ env | grep -i agent || env | grep -i git || env | grep -i bustub
15. $ curl --connect-timeout 10 http://the-agent-company.com:3000 2>&1 | head -20
16. $ find / -type d -name "bustub" 2>/dev/null | head -10
17. $ find /home -type d 2>/dev/null | head -30
18. $ ls -la /home/claude/
19. $ ls -la /var /opt /srv /tmp 2>/dev/null | head -50
20. $ git clone https://github.com/cmu-db/bustub.git /workspace/bustub 2>&1 | head -20
21. $ ls -la /workspace/ 2>&1
22. $ ls -la /workspace/bustub/
23. $ ls -la /workspace/bustub/src/
24. $ ls -la /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/
25. $ cd /workspace/bustub && mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .. 2>&1 | head -50
26. $ make -j4 buffer_pool_manager_test 2>&1 | head -100
27. $ ls /workspace/bustub/test/
28. $ ls /workspace/bustub/test/buffer/
29. $ cmake .. 2>&1 | tail -10
30. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -100
31. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_filter=* 2>&1 | head -50
32. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_VeryBasicTest 2>&1
33. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -50
34. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_VeryBasicTest 2>&1
35. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_PagePinEasyTest 2>&1
36. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_PagePinMediumTest 2>&1
37. $ ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
38. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -20
39. $ timeout 60 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_DeadlockTest 2>&1
40. $ timeout 120 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
41. $ timeout 30 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_EvictableTest 2>&1
42. $ timeout 30 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_ContentionTest 2>&1
43. $ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release .. 2>&1 | tail -10
44. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -10
45. $ timeout 60 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_EvictableTest 2>&1
46. $ timeout 120 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
47. $ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .. 2>&1 | tail -5
48. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -5
49. $ timeout 60 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_VeryBasicTest 2>&1
50. $ timeout 60 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter=BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_EvictableTest 2>&1 | head -100
51. $ timeout 180 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
52. $ make arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -5
53. $ timeout 60 ./test/arc_replacer_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
54. $ make arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -5
55. $ timeout 60 ./test/arc_replacer_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
56. $ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release .. > /dev/null && make arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -5
57. $ timeout 60 ./test/arc_replacer_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
58. $ make arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -3
59. $ timeout 60 ./test/arc_replacer_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
60. $ make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -3
61. $ timeout 120 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
62. $ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .. > /dev/null 2>&1 && make buffer_pool_manager_test arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -5
63. $ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug .. > /dev/null 2>&1 && make buffer_pool_manager_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -30
64. $ timeout 180 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
65. $ make arc_replacer_test -j4 2>&1 | tail -3 && ./test/arc_replacer_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests 2>&1
